rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.note: source material, not LLM-side prior art

DIRECT TWIN: 'What Do LLM Agents Do When Left Alone?' — agents given agency without specific task; identical no-goal baseline-measurement protocol for default unprompted behavior; candidate's 'no explicit goal or reward, only seed prompt, measure where default drift goes' is essentially this exact paper's methodology
exploration/exploitation errors with explicit reward signals; candidate explicitly forbids reward shaping
strategy-GUIDED exploration is opposite of zero-steering
measures drift FROM a goal, presupposes a goal exists; candidate has no goal at all
efficient exploration in LLM-RL with reward; candidate forbids reward
reliability/drift in multi-agent systems with explicit tasks; not no-goal baseline
stateful drift detection problem; different premise

fail_reason: Result #2 (arxiv 2509.21224 'What Do LLM Agents Do When Left Alone?') is a direct twin: same protocol of deploying LLM agents with no specific task to measure spontaneous/default behavior. Both semantic (~0.93) and functional (~0.94) scores far exceed 0.7 threshold. Candidate explicitly acknowledges this in novelty_claim ('what LLMs do when left alone still has some steering') but the distinction (initial seed prompt only vs. default tools) is too thin to constitute novelty — both protocols are no-goal baseline measurements of default agent drift. total_hits=1 >= 1 triggers FAIL.
